creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.
contentSnippet: TBPN, Silicon Valley's cult-favorite tech podcast, will operate independently, even as it's overseen by chief political operative Chris Lehane.

title: (No AI) Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
author: Elizabeth Lopatto
content:
![Elon Musk smiling with big green arrow and Space X rockets.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STKB355_SPACE_X_IPO_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should all [mark our calendars for June 7th](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/6-7_meme) as an IPO date just in case.

Based on the April 1st filing, and the general length of an SEC review before the S-1 document becomes public, the earliest I am expecting a SpaceX IPO is June. (At least, assuming there is still anyone left at the SEC who wants to do their job instead of just glance at the first page of the filing, say, "Seems fine!" and then go out for a smoke break.) Of course, [this process could …](https://www.reuters.com/business/finance/filing-first-trade-inside-us-ipo-process-2026-04-01/)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/906027/elon-musk-lawsuit-ipo-spacex-tesla)

To be honest, I thought Elon Musk would confidentially file for SpaceX's IPO on the 20th of this month, rather than the 1st. But maybe that just means he's moved on to other numbers, and we should all mark our calendars for June 7th as an IPO date just in case.
Based on the April 1st filing, and the general length of an SEC review before the S-1 document becomes public, the earliest I am expecting a SpaceX IPO is June. (At least, assuming there is still anyone left at the SEC who wants to do their job instead of just glance at the first page of the filing, say, "Seems fine!" and then go out for a smoke break.) Of course, this process could  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI just bought TBPN
author: Hayden Field
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/STK155_OPEN_AI_CVirginia__C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

OpenAI has purchased [TBPN](https://www.tbpn.com/), an online talk show that often interviews AI executives and other tech leaders. The show goes live every weekday at 2PM PT, often for a three-hour duration, counting OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, as well as executives from Meta, Microsoft, Palantir, and Andreessen Horowitz, among its past guests, and Bloomberg, CNBC, and Fox Business as its competitors.

TBPN's livestream is primarily available on [X](https://x.com/tbpn) and [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@TBPNLive), but many users watch it on X. OpenAI's purchase comes as a lawsuit between Altman and Elon Musk, who was a co-founder of OpenAI before splitting from the project and now owns X, is headed to trial later this mont …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/906022/openai-buys-tbpn)

title: (No AI) OpenAI acquires TBPN
content: OpenAI acquires TBPN to accelerate global conversations around AI and support independent media, expanding dialogue with builders, businesses, and the broader tech community.
contentSnippet: OpenAI acquires TBPN to accelerate global conversations around AI and support independent media, expanding dialogue with builders, businesses, and the broader tech community.

creator: Molly Taft
title: (No AI) A New Google-Funded Data Center Will Be Powered by a Massive Gas Plant
dc:creator: Molly Taft
content: Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.
contentSnippet: Documents show that one of Google’s new data centers would be powered by a natural gas plant that emits millions of tons of emissions each year—an increasingly common trend in the industry.
